evals.prompt: I have 3 text examples I want to label for sentiment. The texts are: 'I am happy', 'This is terrible', 'It was okay I guess'. The labels should be positive or negative. Write the labeling file so I can use karat label to label them.
evals.expected_output: A valid JSON file using the unified fields format with a fields array containing a display field for the text and a label field for sentiment with labels ["positive", "negative"]. The examples array should have 3 items with the text values filled in but sentiment labels empty/absent (not pre-populated). The agent should also tell the user the karat label command to run.
expectations: Output contains valid JSON with a 'fields' array and an 'examples' array
There is at least one field without 'labels' (display field) for the input text
There is exactly one field with 'labels' containing 'positive' and 'negative'
The examples array has exactly 3 items
The example texts match: 'I am happy', 'This is terrible', 'It was okay I guess'
Sentiment labels are NOT pre-populated on the examples
The agent tells the user to run 'karat label' with a file path
